text: Isomerism is a fundamental concept in organic chemistry but remains a rare occurrence in covalent organic frameworks (COFs). Herein, we rationally designed and controllably synthesized topological isomers of 3D COFs by using an unprecedented tetrahedral unit as precursor at different solvents conditions to afford two isomeric COFs denoted as JUC-620 and JUC-621 featuring **dia** and **qtz** topological networks, respectively. Powder X-ray diffraction, transmission electron microscopy, and other physical characterization confirm the structure of these isomers. Notably, despite sharing identical tetrahedral unit and functional groups, JUC-621 endowed with **qtz**-net possesses permanent mesoporous structure with larger mesopore size (~23 Å) and much larger surface area (~2060 m² g⁻¹) than the microporous isomeric analogue JUC-620 featuring **dia**-net (~12 Å and 980 m² g⁻¹). Furthermore, as-prepared JUC-621 with larger mesopore sizes exhibits fast and efficient uptake for textiles contaminants such as dye molecules and exhibits even higher iodine uptake (6.7 g g⁻¹) than that of JUC-620 (2.9 g g⁻¹), increasing by 2.3 times. The successful synthesis of topological isomers of 3D-COFs not only opens up a unique synthetic route but also gives access to a wider structural diversity as well as potential functionalities in crystalline porous materials.
